key_insight: In portable ultra-low-field MRI for multiple sclerosis, the pseudo-label assisted nnU-Net (PLAn) produced the most accurate white matter lesion segmentations among the tested methods.
clinical_relevance: Better lesion segmentation on portable 64 mT MRI could improve standardized monitoring of MS disability and support more accessible imaging workflows.
method: Paired same-day 64 mT portable MRI and 3T MRI scans from 84 adults with MS or suspected MS were analyzed.
Four automated segmentation approaches were compared: MIMoSA, WMH-SynthSeg, nnU-Net, and PLAn.
Models were evaluated against manual white matter lesion masks using Dice Similarity Coefficient and related to clinical disability measures.
evidence_signal: PLAn-FL achieved the highest segmentation accuracy on pULF scans (mean DSC 0.50 ± 0.24), outperforming the other methods with statistically significant differences.
watch_item: This is a single-cohort evaluation in a modest sample, so broader validation is needed before routine deployment across sites, scanners, and patient populations.
paper_title: Portable Ultra-Low Field MRI Deep-Learning Algorithms for White Matter Lesion Segmentation Improve Accuracy and Reflect Clinical Disability in Multiple Sclerosis
